notes: Hardcoded fallback JWT secret ('supersecretkey') is a significant production risk. No rate limiting on auth endpoints enables brute force. No try/catch around bcrypt.hash/compare or jwt.sign — unhandled exceptions leak stack traces. Token generation logic duplicated across register/login (DRY violation). No input type validation — non-string username/password could cause unexpected behavior. No request size or username length limits. ID generation via users.length+1 breaks on deletion. Good points: consistent error responses, no password leakage, same error for bad user/bad password (prevents enumeration), clean readable structure, proper HTTP status codes.
